𝗦𝗡𝗢𝗪𝗜𝗙𝗬 | To provide a desktop music listening experience by streaming audio from YouTube Music, offering advanced features and a clean interface without ads or account requirements.  🔥 Super Hot!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a desktop music listening experience by streaming audio from YouTube Music, offering advanced features and a clean interface without ads or account requirements.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Search YouTube Music
✅ Stream audio with playback controls
✅ Manage music queues
✅ Create and manage playlists
✅ View synced lyrics
✅ Follow artists and view their pages
✅ Watch music videos
✅ Discord Rich Presence integration
✅ Cloud sync for library
✅ Customizable themes


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 72 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 6 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 2 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Snowify is a desktop music player built using Electron that streams audio directly from YouTube Music. It offers a clean user interface without requiring accounts or displaying ads, providing features like search, playback controls, playlist management, and synced lyrics.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/nyakuoff/Snowify

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔥 𝗙𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗼𝘄 𝘂𝘀 for daily GitHub projects!
👉 GitHub Open Source: https://t.me/GitHub_Open_Source

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃


🏷️ 𝗧𝗔𝗚𝗦
#desktop #electron #music #music_player

𝗔𝗨𝗧𝗢𝗖𝗢𝗠𝗣𝗟𝗘𝗧𝗘 | Enhancing command-line productivity by providing intelligent autocompletion for terminal commands.  ✨ Notable!

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Enhancing command-line productivity by providing intelligent autocompletion for terminal commands.
📅 Created: Sep 2020 • Updated: Mar 2026

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ IDE-style autocompletion for terminal commands
✅ Supports hundreds of popular CLIs (git, npm, docker, aws, etc.)
✅ Contextually relevant suggestions for subcommands, options, and arguments
✅ Declarative schema for defining completions ('completion specs')
✅ Extensible with custom completion specs
✅ Works with various terminals on macOS, Linux, and Windows


⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 25.1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 5.5K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 142 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Amazon Q Developer CLI, formerly Fig, provides IDE-style autocompletion for terminal commands. It enhances the command-line experience by offering contextually relevant suggestions for subcommands, options, and arguments for hundreds of popular CLIs.

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/withfig/autocomplete

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

𝗡𝗘𝗥𝗗𝗟𝗢𝗚 | Reading system logs from one or more remote Linux hosts efficiently, with a focus on web service backend logs, enabling quick filtering by time range and patterns.  🔥 Super Hot!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐹 Go 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Reading system logs from one or more remote Linux hosts efficiently, with a focus on web service backend logs, enabling quick filtering by time range and patterns.
📅 Created: Apr 2025 • Updated: Mar 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Remote-first, multi-host TUI log viewer
✅ No central server required
✅ Log analysis performed on remote nodes
✅ Interactive timeline histogram for visual insight
✅ Efficient querying even on large log files
✅ Gzipped data transfer for bandwidth saving
✅ Supports arbitrary commands for SSH sessions (e.g., Teleport)


⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.5K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 39 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 14 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Nerdlog is a fast, remote-first TUI log viewer designed for efficiently querying and visualizing logs from multiple hosts simultaneously. It requires no central server and performs log analysis on remote nodes, downloading only necessary data for display and timeline histograms.

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/dimonomid/nerdlog

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗢𝗥𝗔 | Organizing and synchronizing web links across multiple devices (Android and desktop) with an optional self-hosted sync server.  🔥 Super Hot!

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🤖 Kotlin 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Organizing and synchronizing web links across multiple devices (Android and desktop) with an optional self-hosted sync server.
📅 Created: Jun 2023 • Updated: Mar 2026

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Organize links with folders, subfolders, and tags
✅ Multiple view layouts with AMOLED theme support
✅ Highlight important links and archive old ones
✅ Customize link names and auto-recognize images/titles
✅ Share from other apps and add folders to Panels
✅ Sort, search, import/export data (JSON, HTML) with auto-backups
✅ Sync links across devices with optional self-hostable server
✅ Browser extension for direct saving


◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 804 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 29 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 33 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Linkora is a local-first link organizer for Android and desktop that allows users to save, organize, and sync links across devices. It supports features like folders, tags, and self-hosted synchronization, along with a browser extension for easy saving.

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
